rules.title: G:\ — Shared Network Drive (Final Outputs Only)
rules.body: The G:\ is accessible by other members of ACHI and needs to contain only final outputs, methodology, and notes or research.
list: Analytics/Research team project folders: G:\DATA\AAA Analytic Projects

rules.title: R:\ — Analytics Team Drive (Working Files)
rules.body: The R:\ is accessible only by the Analytics/Research team and may contain any analyzable datasets, raw output, and programs.
list: May contain analyzable datasets, raw output, and programs

rules.title: Folder Creation & Version Management
rules.body: For each project created, the primary analyst assigned to that project is responsible for creating folders on G:\ and R:\ if they have not yet been created.
list: Each folder should contain a 'previous versions' subfolder to hold everything but the most current version
All work (including works in progress) must be stored on G:\ or R:\
Exception: working datasets may be stored on the SSDs

rules.title: SAS setup for efficiently running your code
rules.body: Each analyst's computer should have four encrypted solid-state drives (SSDs). The SSDrive must point to a different folder than where SASWORK tables are saved — each project gets its own named subfolder. Trial and error has suggested the following drive configuration works most efficiently:
list: Second largest SSD (not C:\) — dedicated solely to the work library and SASWORK. Edit the -WORK line in sasv9.cfg located at C:\Program Files\SASHome\SASFoundation\9.4\nls\en  →  -WORK "E:\SASWORK\SAS Temporary Files"
While in sasv9.cfg, also set (if not already present): -MEMSIZE 58G  |  -SORTSIZE 48G  |  -SUMSIZE 58G  |  -CPUCOUNT 60
Remaining SSD — SSDdrive for project working datasets, one named subfolder per project

callout.title: Back up sasv9.cfg before editing
callout.body: Before changing the config file be sure to save a backup copy of the original.

rules.title: All projects follow a consistent numbered step layout
rules.body: The 'what it should do' part will vary by project but this is a general guide for standard projects.

rows: Library setup, macro definitions, Excel imports — nothing analytical

rules.title: File versioning standard
rules.body: Consistent versioning keeps the team aligned on which program is current.
list: New iterations: append v0.x YYYYMMDD to the filename
Move the prior version into an 'old versions' subfolder
Never overwrite existing programs in place
